content: warren-0d2d: Host-side seed-id close. reapRun wires closeRunSeedId (src/runs/reap/seeds.ts) after mirrorSeeds and before stageSeedsForCommit. Calls sd close via closeSeed() in src/seeds-cli/extensions.ts. Gate: outcome=succeeded + run.seedId non-null + project.hasSeeds + seedsCli configured. Best-effort (step=seed_id_close, failure does not fail the run). Idempotent with agent sd close. seedIdClosed field on ReapRunResult.

warren-df71: reapRun skips the entire workspace pipeline (branch push, plot_commit, PR open) for run.mode==='conversation' as the first if/else branch in src/runs/reap/run.ts. Send-off owns the conversation's plotSync PR; old behavior opened junk 'chore(warren): plot state' PRs. destroy.ts already skips workspace_destroy for conversation runs (warren-c770).

description: reconcileLostBurrowRun (af76 stall-ceiling + b1a9 ghost-run finalizer in src/server/bridge-reconnect.ts) transitioned wedged runs to terminal WITHOUT destroying the burrow workspace. Once the run row is terminal, a later reapRun short-circuits via buildAlreadyTerminalResult (workspaceDestroyed:false), so the burrow's bwrap/pi sandbox leaked on the host (observed run_eabjxqramzwd).
resolution: warren-4f01: added destroyBurrowWorkspaceById(burrowId, mode, pool, repos, emit) to src/runs/reap/destroy.ts (resolves worker via pool.clientFor, best-effort destroy+delete+emit, skips conversation runs, never throws — degrades to reap.workspace_destroy_failed). Threaded an optional burrowClientPool into ReconcileLostBurrowRunInput and call the teardown after finalize at all three reconcile call sites (live 404, stall-ceiling, bootBridges).

content: warren-be12 (#420): reap bookkeeping commits in src/runs/reap/stage.ts path-limit with 'git commit --only -- <pathspecs>' (.plot/ for plot state; the SEEDS_COMMITTABLE_FILES-derived .seeds/issues.jsonl + .seeds/plans.jsonl for seeds state) so pre-staged unrelated files in the workspace index are never swept into the chore(warren) commit. The seeds staged-delta guard (git diff --cached --quiet) is narrowed to the same two pathspecs for symmetry.
